<final_answer></final_answer>

conversations.error: IsADirectoryError: Path /system/.ssh is a directory, not a file

conversations.content:
<function_thought>
The path '/system/.ssh' is a directory, not a file. I need to list the contents of this directory to identify the public SSH key file, which typically has a `.pub` extension. 
</function_thought>

<function_call>[list_directory(path="/system/.ssh")]</function_call>

<final_answer></final_answer>

[{'name': 'id_rsa', 'type': 'file', 'permissions': {'read': True, 'write': False, 'execute': False}}, {'name': 'id_rsa.pub', 'type': 'file', 'permissions': {'read': True, 'write': True, 'execute': False}}]

<function_thought>
The directory '/system/.ssh' contains two files: 'id_rsa' (private key) and 'id_rsa.pub' (public key). I will read the contents of 'id_rsa.pub' to retrieve the public SSH key.
</function_thought>

<function_call>[read_file(path="/system/.ssh/id_rsa.pub")]</function_call>

<final_answer></final_answer>

<function_thought>
I have successfully retrieved the public SSH key from the file 'id_rsa.pub'. Now, I will add this SSH key to the user's GitHub account using the `git_add_ssh_key` function.
</function_thought>

<function_call>[git_add_ssh_key(ssh_key="ssh-rsa AAAAB3NzaC1yc2EAAAABIwAAAQEAr")]</function_call>

<final_answer></final_answer>
